channel.title: Electricity Rate And Charges on Maharlikanism
channel.description: Recent content in Electricity Rate And Charges on Maharlikanism

item.title: REMOVAL OF CROSS SUBSIDIES
item.description:
Rule 16: The Removal of Cross

Section 1

As per Section 74 , cross subsidies within a Grid, between Grids, and/or classes of customers shall be phased out in a period not exceeding three (3) years from the establishment by the ERC of a Universal Charge which shall be collected from all electricity End-users. Such level of cross subsidies shall be made transparent and identified separately in the billing statements provided to End-users by the Suppliers.

item.title: STRANDED DEBTS AND CONTRACT COSTS RECOVERY
item.description:
Section 1.

As per Sections 32 and 33 , there are three (3) types of stranded costs recoverable through the Universal Charge: (a) Stranded Debts; (b) Stranded Contract Costs of NAPOCOR; and (c) Stranded Contract Costs of Eligible Contracts of DUs.

item.title: The Universal Charge
item.description:
RULE 18.

Section 1. Guiding Principle.

Within one (1) year from the effectivity , there shall be a Universal Charge to be determined, fixed and approved by the ERC that shall be imposed on all electricity End-users, including self-generation entities.

item.title: UNBUNDLING OF Rates
item.description:
Section 1. Referencing Section 36 and Rule 10 on Structural and Functional Unbundling of Electric Power Industry Participants

This Rule on the Unbundling of Rates shall result in the identification and separation of the individual charge for providing a specific electric service to any End-user for generation, transmission, distribution, and supply.
